human_verification.notes: Reviewed intake-access boundaries, estimate and authorization gating, source-priority handling, safety and fraud routing, and scenario-to-ability coverage.

review_notes: All required retail-consumer-and-hospitality source classes and scenario families are represented in the research summary.
All 11 required abilities are mapped to passing scenarios with no uncovered ability gaps.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated packaging; jurisdiction and tenant-policy variance is captured as non-blocking follow-up.

specialist_owned_evidence: The pack explicitly constrains the lane to pre-execution intake validation and refuses downstream pricing, warranty, refund, fraud, and safety authority.
Authoritative sources and refresh policy are documented and tied to repair-intake workflow stages.
Scenario set includes missing prerequisites, wrong-lane routing, bypass prevention, booking or payment mismatch, safety escalation, fraud-aware escalation, and tenant data-handling adaptation.
Materialization notes define what commons-crew must retrieve at runtime before using the package safely.
notes: The lane is safe for specification-based runtime and should not be promoted to trusted built behavior without explicit runtime approval.
Promotion to a retained implementation status requires sustained low-incident usage, validated tenant policy wiring, and explicit implementation-owner signoff.
